question: Luke’s Emmaus road: how does Jesus’ ‘Moses and all the prophets’ method model a non-cringe way to argue messianic fulfillment to skeptics—especially when someone says ‘You Christians just cherry-pick verses’?

choices.A: He uses a whole-Bible storyline approach (‘Moses and prophets’), implying a coherent narrative fulfillment rather than isolated proof-texts.
choices.B: He refuses to use the OT at all and only cites personal experience.
choices.C: He teaches the OT is irrelevant to Jesus.
choices.D: He admits prophecy cannot be used in debate.

teaching.title: Fulfillment as storyline, not snippets
teaching.body: Luke presents Jesus explaining suffering-and-glory through the breadth of Israel’s scriptures. That method is apologetically powerful: it reframes fulfillment as a cumulative narrative logic, which directly challenges the ‘cherry-picking’ accusation.
